title: Functions, Multiple Results, Closures, Recursion, and Contracts · Independent Go Trial
summary: A rule engine needs small composable functions, explicit failure results, isolated stateful closures, and bounded recursive traversal. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a composable rule and transformation library with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design function signatures, named types, parameters, results, and first-class function values around one responsibility.
Use multiple results and the blank identifier without discarding failure or evidence that the caller needs.
Define and call variadic functions, distinguish the slice parameter, and forward existing slices with explicit ownership.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.instruction:
lab case — Build 3 independent rule closures with thresholds 15 and 21; preserve multiple-result failure evidence, forward one variadic slice deliberately, and prove recursion reaches a bounded base case.
